cases.mutation: Preserves the public interface but never advances the internal state on valid clock edges.
Advances by two states per rising clock edge, skipping half of the required permutation.
Uses a monotonic output mapping instead of the required non-monotonic out2,out0,out3,out1 permutation.
Forces the same terminal state on every valid clock edge.
Drives a plausible voltage output but violates one-hot rotation by keeping only out0 high.
Matches the first public sequence window but ignores asynchronous reset assertions between clock edges, so the hidden post-reset window does not restart.
